title: Ormat Announces Pricing of Public Offering of Common Stock on Behalf of Stockholder Orix Corporation
author: Globe Newswire
description: Ormat Technologies announced the pricing of a secondary offering of 3.7 million shares of its common stock on behalf of ORIX Corporation. Ormat is not offering any shares and will not receive any proceeds from the sale.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The article states that Ormat Technologies is not offering any shares and will not receive any proceeds from the sale, indicating a neutral sentiment as the company is not directly involved in the offering.
ORIX Corporation is the selling stockholder in the secondary offering, indicating a positive sentiment as the company is taking advantage of the opportunity to sell its shares.

title: Ormat Announces Pricing of Public Offering of Common Stock on Behalf of Stockholder Orix Corporation
author: N/A
description: Ormat Technologies, Inc. announced the pricing of an underwritten secondary offering of 3,700,000 shares of its common stock on behalf of ORIX Corporation. The offering is expected to close on December 13, 2024.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The article is neutral as it simply announces the pricing of a secondary offering, which is a common corporate action and does not indicate any significant positive or negative news about the company's operations or financial performance.
The article is neutral towards ORIX Corporation as it is simply the selling stockholder in the secondary offering, and the article does not provide any additional information about the company.

title: Nikkei Index Revisits Glory Days, Surges To 33-Year Highs: 5 US-Listed Japanese Stocks To Watch
author: Piero Cingari
description:
The Nikkei 225 stock market index in Japan is seeing a significant rebound in 2023, recalling memories of its glory days when Japanese technology stocks dominated the global scene.
With a 25% year-to-date increase as of June 6 and at its highest level since July 1990 (almost 33 years ago), Japanese equities have once again piqued the interest of foreign investors.
Factors Driving the Rally:  Several major variables have contributed to the Nikkei's comeback. For instance, Japanese exporters benefit from a historically weak Japanese yen, which strengthens their global competitiveness. Furthermore, the appealing and low valuations of Japanese equities are attracting the attention of value-style investors looking for opportunities in an undervalued market.
Furthermore, Warren Buffett's endorsement of Japanese equities has considerably increased the Japanese market's global attractiveness. His multiple bets on Japanese "sogo sosha," or trading house enterprises, have ...
